title: Sample Retrieval Policy Configuration
description: Reference configuration for RAG retrieval logic - Technology-agnostic template

customer_facing_chatbot.description: Public-facing customer service chatbot - STRICT FILTERING

mandatory_filters.description: MANDATORY: Exclude all placeholders (legal liability protection)

safe_for_client_direct_use.description: Prefer chunks safe for direct customer use

use_case_multipliers.description_placeholder: Heavy downrank (should be filtered out anyway, but extra safety)
use_case_multipliers.description_safe: Downrank chunks not safe for direct use

customer_facing_chatbot.description_top_k: Retrieve top-10 chunks for answer generation

required_disclaimers.plan_specific: Check your policy documents or contact customer service for plan-specific details
required_disclaimers.network_verification: Verify provider network status before scheduling appointment
required_disclaimers.operational_process: Contact customer service for specific instructions
required_disclaimers.policy_interpretation: Policy interpretation may vary, consult your policy documents

fallback_behavior.no_results: I don't have information on that topic. Please contact customer service at [number]
fallback_behavior.placeholder_only: For accurate information, please contact customer service at [number]
fallback_behavior.description: If only placeholders retrieved (should not happen due to filtering), provide fallback

answer_safety.weakest_link_rule: Overall answer confidence = MIN(confidence of all retrieved chunks)

internal_knowledge_assistant.description: Internal employee knowledge assistant - MODERATE FILTERING
internal_knowledge_assistant.description_mandatory: No mandatory filters - allow all content

status.description: Optionally exclude deprecated (can show with labeling)

use_case_multipliers.description_placeholder: Downrank placeholders but allow (with warning labels)
use_case_multipliers.description_safe: Internal users can handle unsafe-for-client content
use_case_multipliers.description_confidence: Small penalty for low confidence (internal users can judge)
use_case_multipliers.description_mapping: Mapping useful for internal users (show relationships)

placeholder_labeling.label: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER - Not confirmed product data
placeholder_labeling.description: If chunk has is_placeholder=true, add warning label

internal_knowledge_assistant.description_top_k: Retrieve top-20 chunks (more context for internal users)

required_disclaimers.placeholder_present: Note: This answer includes placeholder content (not confirmed product data)

fallback_behavior.no_results: No information found. Check data directory or escalate to knowledge team.

self_service_faq_portal.description: Self-service FAQ portal - VERY STRICT FILTERING
self_service_faq_portal.user_audience: Customers browsing FAQ without human assistance

mandatory_filters.description: ONLY FAQ Approved with High confidence (no Rules, no Mapping, no Placeholders)

self_service_faq_portal.rationale: Self-service means no human to add disclaimers, so only highest-confidence content allowed

use_case_multipliers.description: Eliminate anything not FAQ Approved High confidence

self_service_faq_portal.description_top_k: Retrieve top-5 FAQ Approved chunks only

required_disclaimers.general: For personalized assistance, contact customer service at [number]

fallback_behavior.no_results: This question requires personalized assistance. Please contact customer service at [number]

developer_testing.description_mandatory: No filters - retrieve everything to validate ingestion and test placeholder handling

retrieval_weighting.description: No use-case multipliers - test raw weights

developer_testing.description_top_k: Retrieve top-50 chunks for comprehensive testing
developer_testing.warning: NEVER deploy 'no filtering' to customer-facing environment

weight_calculation.description: Detailed weight calculation ensuring trust hierarchy

use_case_multiplier.source: Penalty based on use case (see use_cases section above)

example_1.description: FAQ-001 'What is a deductible?' matches query well

example_2.description: PLAN-003 'Basic Family Plan' placeholder heavily downranked (should be filtered out anyway)

comparison.conclusion: FAQ dominates despite identical semantic match, trust hierarchy enforced

chunk_type_preferences.description: Typical ranking preferences by chunk type

tier_1_primary.use: Direct customer answers with high confidence

tier_3_placeholder.use: Question routing, context awareness ONLY, NEVER direct customer answers

placeholder_penalties.description: Additional penalties for placeholder chunks across all use cases
placeholder_penalties.description_cap: Placeholder base weight capped at 0.3 max (even if metadata says higher)

status_based_filtering.description: How to handle different status values

Needs Review.customer_facing: Yes, but add caution disclaimer for compliance topics (e.g., RULE-008)
Needs Review.note: Requires compliance review for certain rules

Pending Detail.customer_facing: Yes, but add operational disclaimer (e.g., FAQ-011 refill prescriptions)
Pending Detail.note: Operational details incomplete, defer specifics to customer service

mapping_handling.retrieval: Allow in retrieval (useful for finding related FAQ/Rules)
mapping_handling.display: NEVER display mapping text directly to customers
mapping_handling.use: Use mapping to identify related chunks, then retrieve and display those
mapping_handling.customer_facing: Retrieve for context, but exclude from answer text

validation_scenarios.description: Test scenarios to validate retrieval policy implementation

scenario_2.query: Does my Basic Family Plan cover dental?
scenario_2.expected_behavior: PLAN-003 placeholder filtered out OR heavily downranked (position >20)

scenario_3.expected_behavior: PROV-001 placeholder filtered out OR heavily downranked

scenario_4.query: How do I refill a prescription?

monitoring_and_tuning.description: Metrics to monitor and weight tuning guidance

chunk_type_distribution.metric: % of each chunk type in top-10 results

placeholder_ranking.metric: Average position of placeholders when they appear
placeholder_ranking.target: Position >20 (ideally not in top-20 at all)

placeholder_leak_rate.metric: % of customer answers with placeholder in sources

answer_quality_by_source.metric: User satisfaction by dominant source type

step_1.details: Run 50-100 sample queries, record top-10 chunk types and scores

step_2.details: Placeholders too high? FAQ not dominating? Rules never appearing?

step_3.details: Change weights by 10-20% at a time (e.g., placeholder 0.2 → 0.1 or FAQ 1.0 → 1.1)
step_3.caution: Don't make large changes at once, measure impact iteratively

step_4.details: Run validation scenarios, ensure placeholder leak rate 0%, ensure answer quality acceptable

placeholders_in_top_10.issue: Placeholders appearing in top-10 despite low weights
solution: Ensure pre-retrieval filtering excludes placeholders for customer-facing
Review Rules content for semantic relevance

mapping_noise.issue: Mapping text appearing in customer answers
solution: Ensure mapping excluded from direct display (implementation issue, not weight issue)
Keep mapping weights 0.4-0.6 for cross-reference only

implementation_notes.technology_agnostic: This policy is technology-agnostic. Implementation teams implement filtering and weighting logic in their chosen RAG framework.
reference_specifications: CHUNK_FILTERING_RULES.md - Detailed filtering rules by use case
RETRIEVAL_WEIGHTING_SPEC.md - Weight calculation and hierarchy enforcement
critical_requirements: Weight formula MUST enforce trust hierarchy (FAQ > Rules > Mapping > Placeholders)
Disclaimers MUST be added for plan/network-specific questions
Traceability MUST be maintained (answer → chunks → source files)
Monitoring MUST detect placeholder leaks (0% leak rate target)
